providers.name: adzuna-dk
providers.notes: Adzuna DK — general-purpose Danish job aggregator (free tier). Register at https://developer.adzuna.com to get app_id and app_key, paste them into queryParams above, then flip enabled: true. Free tier: ~250 calls/day.

providers.name: jobnet
providers.notes: jobnet.dk's public-facing search no longer returns JSON for unauthenticated callers. Real machine integration against STAR requires certificate auth and a paid agreement (spoc@star.dk). Left here as a config skeleton; flip to enabled: true only if you have a working endpoint.

providers.name: jobindex
providers.notes: jobindex.dk has no public API and automation may violate its ToS. To include listings, export or copy results from the browser and drop a CSV or JSON file at data/imports/jobindex-*.csv with columns: title, company, location, url, description, posted_at.

providers.name: linkedin
providers.notes: LinkedIn requires a logged-in session. Use job alerts or a manual export.

providers.name: html-example
providers.notes: Requires AngleSharp. The exact selectors above are placeholders — update for the target site.

providers.name: jobindex-rss
providers.notes: Jobindex.dk public RSS feed (undocumented but stable as of 2026-05). Returns latest postings; q= accepts space-separated keywords matched as OR-of-words. Items expose title, link, pubDate, and a short description; full body requires fetching the link. The same backend powers it-jobbank.dk/jobsoegning.rss and ofir.dk (which now 301-redirects to jobindex.dk). Run only one of jobindex-rss / it-jobbank-rss as enabled to avoid heavy duplicates. Keep rateLimitRps low; this feed is unadvertised and ToS does not bless automation.

providers.name: it-jobbank-rss
providers.notes: IT-jobbank.dk public RSS feed (undocumented but stable as of 2026-05). Same Jobindex backend as jobindex-rss, but pre-scoped to IT-only inventory. q= accepts space-separated keywords matched as OR-of-words. Heavy overlap with jobindex-rss — enable one or the other, not both. Keep rateLimitRps low; this feed is unadvertised and ToS does not bless automation.

providers.name: jobsearch-dk
providers.notes: Jobsearch.dk — open RSS 2.0 feed, ~100 newest postings, no auth. Items expose title / description / link only (no pubDate, no company/location fields). Same backend as Jobzonen.dk. Enable jobsearch-dk or jobzonen, not both — they produce the same listing pool. jobsearch-dk is the canonical pick.

providers.name: careerjet-dk
providers.notes: Careerjet DK — Danish coverage of the global Careerjet aggregator (free tier). Register at https://www.careerjet.com/partners/api to get an affid, paste it into queryParams above, then flip enabled: true. There is no per-job id in the response, so url is used as the dedupe key. Pagination is page-based and 1-indexed; maxPages: 5 caps fetches at 500 jobs total.

providers.name: jooble
providers.notes: Jooble.org — global aggregator with Danish coverage (free tier). Register at https://jooble.org/api/about to obtain an api_key, paste it into queryParams.api_key (it gets substituted into {api_key} in the endpoint path), then flip enabled: true. Pagination is page-based and 1-indexed; maxPages: 5 caps fetches at 250 jobs total. The page param is injected into bodyTemplate per request since this is a POST endpoint.

providers.name: recruit-it
providers.notes: Small Danish IT recruitment agency (~tens of roles). The hiring company is embedded in the title (e.g. 'IT-chef til 3F') rather than a separate field, so staticFields.company stamps the agency name. Location is rendered as a plain text node next to an icon with no wrapper class, so locationSelector is intentionally omitted. Note: linkSelector ':scope' relies on AngleSharp accepting :scope — verify with a smoke test before flipping enabled: true.

providers.name: stepstone-dk
providers.notes: stepstone.dk has no public RSS or consumer-facing API as of 2026-05. api.stepstone.com is a partner job-distribution system for ATS vendors, not for consuming listings, and requires a business contract. To include Stepstone.dk listings, export results from the browser and drop a CSV/JSON at data/imports/stepstone-*.csv with columns: title, company, location, url, description, posted_at.
